headline: Wholesale and Retail Sector Sees 8,200 Shop Closures in 2025
context: The Malaysian Retailers Association reported 8,200 physical retail store closures in 2025, up 34% from 2024, driven by e-commerce growth, rising rents, and consumer spending compression among M40 households.

cards.big: 8,200 shops closed in one year — 34% jump in retail casualties as Shopee and Lazada capture 28% of consumer spending
cards.sub: Average mall occupancy in Klang Valley fell to 72% — every 4th retail lot sits empty

cards.big: E-commerce grew to RM52 billion in 2025 — capturing spending that previously sustained 142,000 physical retail stores
cards.sub: Retail employment fell by 34,000 jobs while e-commerce warehousing created only 12,000 — net loss of 22,000 positions

cards.big: Small retailers in shophouses employ 680,000 workers — overwhelmingly family businesses serving local communities
cards.sub: Average shoplot rent in Petaling Jaya rose 18% despite declining footfall — landlords slow to adjust expectations

cards.big: No digital economy tax equalisation exists — Shopee and Lazada marketplace sellers undercut physical stores partly through tax arbitrage
cards.sub: SST collection on e-commerce estimated at 42% compliance versus 89% for physical retail — unfair regulatory playing field

cards.big: When 8,200 shops close while platforms thrive tax-free, the issue isn't competition — it's that the rules favour the disruptor
Should e-commerce platforms be required to collect and remit SST at point of sale to equalise the tax burden with physical retailers?
